articles.title: SPACE TECH FUNDING COMES BACK TO EARTH
articles.summary: Less venture funding dollars are going to space tech startups. In 2021, $12.1 billion went to space tech across 450 deals. In 2022, this decreased by 25% to $9 billion. So far in 2023, we’ve only seen $2.4 billion go to them. To the moon, maybe, maybe not.

articles.title: CHATGPT'S PERFORMANCE IS SLIPPING, APPARENTLY
articles.summary: UC Berkeley researchers found that ChatGPT has not improved over time, and in fact, may have gotten worse. The researchers suggest that one of the reasons for this decline could be the result of new safety protocols being implemented by OpenAI.

articles.title: OTHER PARTNERS DEPART THE FIRM
articles.summary: Five partners are leaving the firm, but the headline is that Mike Moritz is officially moving on after 38 years service. As the venture capital scene gets more competitive, it’ll be interesting to see what 2024 and beyond has in store for Sequoia.

articles.title: YOUR GUIDE TO SAAS PRODUCT METRICS
articles.summary: This article explores the metrics you should be aware of as you build out your product-led growth muscle at your SaaS company. It double-clicks on the funnel conversions you can expect from when a visitor lands on your website and eventually becomes a paying customer.

articles.title: BUILDING AN AN EARLY STAGE STARTUP: LESSONS FROM AKITA SOFTWARE
articles.summary: Gergely, writer at The Pragmatic Engineer, interviews Jean Yang, the founder of Akita, which was very recently acquired by Postman. They dive into finding your co-founder, fundraising, hiring, pivoting, and all the challenges faced along the way.

articles.title: EFFECTIVE > PRODUCTIVE
articles.summary: Optimizing for productivity and being busy doesn’t necessarily mean you’re working on the right things.

articles.title: GAMES
articles.summary: AI has long played a role in games, from procedurally generated environments, to machine learning and NPC behavior. Generative AI is the next frontier in game development, enabling hyper personalization and adaptive environments. How about a game that designs itself and never ends?

articles.title: RICH AND ANONYMOUS
articles.summary: An insightful read that digs into the positive correlation between wealth and social liability. In today’s society, the wealthier you get, the more you feel a responsibility to give back to family, friends, and the global community.

articles.title: FIND YOUR WINNING IDEA BY SELLING PRODUCTS THAT DON’T EXIST YET
articles.summary: The story of Material Security’s path to product-market fit, in which they took a circuitous route where they tested multiple different ideas.

articles.title: SETH GODIN ON GOALS AND EXPECTATIONS
articles.summary: As a founder, you want to get things done fast and see results now. Sometimes this isn’t always possible. Godin provides a nice reminder that we have to be persistently patient.

articles.title: THERE’S NO REBOUND IN SIGHT FOR UNPROFITABLE SAAS
articles.summary: The SaaS industry is facing a profitability crisis, with many companies spending more money on sales and marketing than they earn in revenue.

articles.title: 5 INTERESTING LEARNINGS FROM DATADOG AT ~$2 BILLION IN ARR
articles.summary: File this under “problems we’d like to have” - thinking through being worth 18x ARR with continuing massive growth across every KPI. Fascinating read and something to aspire to.
